informal_statement.text: Given an unweighted directed graph represented by an adjacency list of n vertices and a source vertex, return a vector dist of length n where dist[v] is Some(k) if the shortest path from source to v uses k edges, and None if v is unreachable.
preconditions: every neighbor index is in [0, n)
required_properties: if dist[v] == Some(k), there is a directed path from source to v using exactly k edges
if dist[v] == Some(k), no directed path from source to v uses fewer than k edges
if dist[v] == None, there is no directed path from source to v
edge_cases: graph with a single vertex and no edges

semantic_units.description: Length invariant: dist has length n, equal to the number of vertices.
common_failure_modes: stating an upper bound instead of equality
forgetting to anchor the source distance

semantic_units.description: Reachability witness: if dist[v] = Some(k), there is a directed path from source to v using exactly k edges.
common_failure_modes: stating the weaker 'v is reachable' without length

semantic_units.description: Minimality: if dist[v] = Some(k), no path uses fewer than k edges.
common_failure_modes: confusing the minimal and any-path formulations

semantic_units.description: Unreachability: dist[v] = None iff no path exists from source to v.
common_failure_modes: only stating one direction of the iff
conflating 'no path' with 'path of length > bound'

reference_obligations.nl_gloss: The output has the same length as the number of vertices.

reference_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (adj : Adj) (src : Nat), src < adj.length → (bfsShortestPath adj src).get? src = some (some 0)
reference_obligations.nl_gloss: The distance from the source to itself is zero.

reference_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (adj : Adj) (src v k : Nat), (bfsShortestPath adj src).get? v = some (some k) → ∃ path : List Nat, path.head? = some src ∧ path.getLast? = some v ∧ path.length = k + 1
reference_obligations.nl_gloss: If dist[v] = Some(k), there is a path from source to v with exactly k edges.

reference_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (adj : Adj) (src v k : Nat), (bfsShortestPath adj src).get? v = some (some k) → ∀ (path : List Nat), path.head? = some src → path.getLast? = some v → k + 1 ≤ path.length
reference_obligations.nl_gloss: If dist[v] = Some(k), no path from source to v has fewer than k edges.

reference_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (adj : Adj) (src v : Nat), (bfsShortestPath adj src).get? v = some none ↔ ¬ ∃ (path : List Nat), path.head? = some src ∧ path.getLast? = some v
reference_obligations.nl_gloss: dist[v] = None if and only if no path from source to v exists.

generated_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (n : Nat) (adj : Fin n → List (Fin n)) (s : Fin n), True
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: State the setting: we model the graph as an adjacency list function adj from each vertex to a list of successor vertices, and the source s is a valid vertex in Fin n.
The implementation returns a vector dist of length n (one entry per vertex).

generated_obligations.lean_statement: let dist := {{dist_def}}; dist[s] = some 0 ∧ (∀ v : Fin n, v ≠ s → dist[v] = none ∨ dist[v] = some (Nat.succ 0))
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: At the start (or after the initialization phase), the source has distance 0 and all other vertices are initially unreachable (None), before any relaxation.

Maintain an invariant relating the queue/frontier to vertices whose dist values are already assigned: every vertex in the queue has dist[v] = some k for some k, and that k is the number of edges used by the corresponding BFS layer.

generated_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (state : {{impl_state_type}}) (v : Fin n), dist[v] = some k → ∃ path : List (Fin n), pathStartsAt path s ∧ pathEndsAt path v ∧ pathLengthEdges path = k
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Whenever dist[v] = some k, there exists a directed path from s to v using exactly k edges. (So dist values are always realizable upper bounds on shortest-path lengths.)

generated_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (state : {{impl_state_type}}) (v : Fin n) (k : Nat), dist[v] = some k → (∀ k' : Nat, k' < k → ¬ ∃ path, pathStartsAt path s ∧ pathEndsAt path v ∧ pathLengthEdges path = k')
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: If dist[v] = some k, then no path with fewer than k edges exists. (So assigned distances are minimal.)

generated_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (u v : Fin n) (k : Nat), dist[u] = some k → (v ∈ adj u) → dist[v] = none → after_relaxation dist v = some (k+1)
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: When exploring an edge u→v from a vertex u with known distance k, if v is currently unreachable (None), the algorithm sets dist[v] to k+1.

generated_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (v : Fin n) (k k' : Nat), dist[v] = some k → after_algorithm dist'[v] = some k' → k' = k
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Once a vertex v has been assigned a distance, the algorithm never changes it to a different value; in particular it never overwrites with a larger or smaller distance.

generated_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (v : Fin n), (¬ ∃ path, pathStartsAt path s ∧ pathEndsAt path v) → dist[v] = none
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: If there is no directed path from s to v, then the algorithm returns None for v.

generated_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (v : Fin n), (∃ path, pathStartsAt path s ∧ pathEndsAt path v) → ∃ k : Nat, dist[v] = some k ∧ (∀ k' : Nat, (∃ path', pathStartsAt path' s ∧ pathEndsAt path' v ∧ pathLengthEdges path' = k') → k ≤ k')
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: If v is reachable from s, then dist[v] is Some(k) where k is the length (in edges) of a shortest path: no reachable path uses fewer than k edges.

generated_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (v : Fin n), dist[v] = some k ↔ (∃ path, pathStartsAt path s ∧ pathEndsAt path v ∧ pathLengthEdges path = k) ∧ (∀ k' < k, ¬ ∃ path', pathStartsAt path' s ∧ pathEndsAt path' v ∧ pathLengthEdges path' = k')
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: A combined characterization: dist[v] = Some(k) exactly when k is the minimal number of edges among all s→v paths.

generated_obligations.lean_statement: The algorithm terminates for all finite graphs: ∀ n adj s, ∃ t, run_bfs adj s terminates in t steps
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Prove the BFS implementation terminates (e.g., because each vertex is enqueued at most once when its dist changes from None to Some).
